name: Data-Oriented Design
def: Designs code around how data is laid out and accessed, optimising for cache and bulk transformation.
problem: Beat memory-latency bottlenecks by shaping data for sequential, cache-friendly, vectorisable access.
mechanism: Group hot fields contiguously (Structure of Arrays), process in batches, minimise pointer chasing and per-object polymorphism in hot loops.
benefits: Large speedups from cache locality & SIMD
failures: Optimising cold paths for no gain
Layout churn breaking many call sites
whenNot: Cold/IO-bound code where layout is irrelevant
Small data sets that fit trivially in cache
myths: "DOD = ECS" — ECS is one application; DOD is the broader principle
